description:
## Component: hx-tabs (NEW)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 6/6 libraries**

Tabbed interface for organizing content into switchable panels. Composed of hx-tab (trigger) and hx-tab-panel (content) children.

### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite)
Container component. Drupal controls tab labels and panel content via slots. Component provides tab switching behavior and keyboard navigation.

### API Design
**Properties:** `selected` (index or id of active tab), `orientation` (horizontal|vertical), `activation` (auto|manual — auto activates on focus, manual requires Enter/Space)
**Slots:** Default (accepts hx-tab and hx-tab-panel children)
**CSS Parts:** `tabs`, `tab-list`, `tab-panel`, `indicator` (active tab underline)
**Events:** `hx-tab-change` (composed: true, detail: { index, tab, panel })

### Child Components
- `hx-tab`: Tab trigger button. Properties: `selected`, `disabled`. Slots: default (label), `icon`.
- `hx-tab-panel`: Content panel. Slots: default (panel content — Drupal WYSIWYG, views, etc.)

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Patient record tabs — Drupal controls all panel content #}
<hx-tabs>
  <hx-tab>Demographics</hx-tab>
  <hx-tab>Medical History</hx-tab>
  <hx-tab>Medications</hx-tab>
  <hx-tab>Lab Results</hx-tab>

  <hx-tab-panel>
    {{ content.field_demographics }}
  </hx-tab-panel>
  <hx-tab-panel>
    {{ content.field_medical_history }}
  </hx-tab-panel>
  <hx-tab-panel>
    {{ drupal_view('medications', 'patient_block', node.id) }}
  </hx-tab-panel>
  <hx-tab-panel>
    {{ drupal_view('lab_results', 'patient_block', node.id) }}
  </hx-tab-panel>
</hx-tabs>
```

Panel content is fully slot-driven — each panel can contain Drupal render arrays, Views, Paragraphs, or any CMS content. Component handles tab/panel association by DOM order.

### Healthcare: Patient record sections, department dashboards, form wizard steps

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Tab/panel association by DOM order (1st tab → 1st panel)
2. Only active panel visible (others hidden but in DOM)
3. Keyboard: Arrow keys navigate tabs, Tab moves to panel content
4. Automatic ARIA: tab/tablist/tabpanel roles, aria-selected, aria-controls
5. slotchange listener handles dynamic tab addition/removal
6. Active tab indicator (underline) with smooth animation
7. Vertical orientation option for side-tab layouts
title: T1-16: hx-tabs — Tabbed Content Panels

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 9**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tabs/hx-tab.ts`:
- Around line 65-75: The component currently sets ARIA on the host via
setAttribute calls in _syncHostAria called from connectedCallback; refactor to
use ElementInternals instead: call this.attachInternals() in the constructor and
store the returned internals (e.g., this._internals), then replace setAttribute
manipulations in _syncHostAria (and anywhere aria-selected / aria-disabled are
updated) with updates to this._internals.ariaSelected and
this._internals.ariaDisabled (or use the ARIAMixin API on internals) while
optionally keeping reflected attributes for backward compatibility; ensure
connectedCallback still wires this._handleClick and calls the updated
_syncHostAria that now updates internals rather than setAttribute.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tabs/hx-tabs.stories.ts`:
- Around line 198-199: Replace hardcoded inline visual values in the hx-tabs
story templates with token-driven CSS custom properties using the library
convention (prefixed with --hx-); specifically, find the inline style attributes
(e.g., the long style string containing
margin/font-size/color/padding/background/border-left) in
packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tabs/hx-tabs.stories.ts and swap literal
values like `#6b7280`, `#f3f4f6`, rem/px numbers, and border colors with
corresponding --hx- variables (for example --hx-color-muted, --hx-bg-muted,
--hx-spacing-sm, --hx-font-size-sm, --hx-border-accent); ensure all other
occurrences listed (ranges 305-310, 335-352, 357-383, 385-433, 443-480, 629-633,
652-656, 689-763) are updated to use the --hx-* custom properties so the stories
adhere to the theming contract.
- Around line 278-284: The play functions are querying a non-existent internal
<button>

### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite)
Container component. Drupal controls tab labels and panel content via slots. Component provides tab switching behavior and keyboard navigation.

### API Design
**Properties:** `selected` (index or id of active tab), `o...

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Real bug: `_syncHostAria()` at line 75 sets `tabindex` based solely on `this.selected`, ignoring `this.disabled`. A disabled tab that happens to be selected will receive `tabindex="0"`, making it keyboard-focusable. Per ARIA APG, disabled tabs must always have `tabindex="-1"`. This is a genuine accessibility defect.

`hxTabs!.getAttribute('selected')` at story line 130 is the correct way to test attribute reflection — the `selected` property has `reflect: true`, so the attribute will be `'0'` as a string. This assertion is accurate and appropriate for a play function. No code change warranted.

Story files use inline styles for demonstration purposes — this is standard Storybook practice. The project's "no hardcoded values" rule applies to component implementation files, not to story-level demonstration markup. The hardcoded colors in the ManualActivation info banner are appropriate here.

The test at line 169 correctly documents the intentional architecture: `hx-tabs` owns ARIA wiring for its children including `role="tabpanel"`. Setting `role="tabpanel"` in `hx-tab-panel.connectedCallback` without `aria-labelledby` would create incomplete ARIA. Both attributes are set together by the parent orchestrator by design, and the JSDoc explicitly documents this. All 89 tests pass confirming correctness.

The `await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50))` pattern is the established way to verify that a negative condition (event not fired) holds across a tick. The `oneEvent` helper cannot be used for "nothing happened" tests. This pattern is consistent with other tests in the file (lines 388, 436, 448, etc.) and is acceptable for this use case.

`Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)` for ID generation is non-deterministic and produces a 7-char base36 string (~2.8B combinations). While collision probability is low for UI IDs, a sequential counter is more predictable, produces stable output, and avoids any (however theoretical) collision risk. Critical severity confirms this needs addressing.

The early `return` after clamping forces a double Lit update cycle: first pass clamps and returns (no DOM sync), second pass does actual work. This causes the Lit dev-mode warning "scheduled update after update completed" seen in testing. Removing the early return and using `clampedSelected` for the rest of the method allows syncing to complete in a single pass while still propagating the corrected property value.

`role="tabpanel"` is intentionally set by the parent `hx-tabs` orchestrator because it must be coordinated with `aria-labelledby`. Setting `role="tabpanel"` in `hx-tab-panel.connectedCallback` without `aria-labelledby` would produce incomplete ARIA. The JSDoc explicitly documents this contract. The architecture is correct for this composite widget pattern.

Hardcoded fallback values in CSS `var()` calls (e.g., `var(--hx-color-neutral-800, #1e293b)`) are the established pattern used throughout all component styles in this library. They provide resilience when tokens are unavailable and are consistent with `hx-button`, `hx-card`, `hx-text-input`, and all other components. The project rule targets direct hardcoded values, not `var()` fallbacks.

`pointer-events: none` at line 10 of `hx-tab.styles.ts` is intentionally partial — it blocks mouse interaction. Keyboard interaction prevention is handled separately via `tabindex="-1"` in `_syncHostAria()`. These two mechanisms are complementary and work correctly together. The `pointer-events: none` CSS rule is correct for its purpose.

`font-family: var(--hx-font-family-sans, sans-serif)` at line 7 of `hx-tabs.styles.ts` — `sans-serif` is a CSS generic font family keyword, not a hardcoded value. Using a generic family as a fallback for a custom property token is correct and standard CSS. All other components use this same pattern.

The `@query('.tab-list') private _tabListEl!: HTMLElement` declaration is dead code — `_tabListEl` is never read anywhere in `hx-tabs.ts`. The keyboard handler uses `document.activeElement` and the `_getTabs()` method; the slot change handler calls `_syncTabsAndPanels()`. Per project standards: "No orphaned code — If it's imported nowhere, it doesn't belong. If it's dead, delete it."

error: PR exceeded 2 review iterations. Escalated to CTO.
